seo.title: Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents in Vercel AI SDK Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix sensitive data leakage prevention in ai agents in Vercel AI SDK agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Vercel AI SDK, sensitive data leakage in AI agents often occurs when streaming responses from providers like OpenAI inadvertently include API keys, user PII, or internal secrets due to hallucinations or unfiltered tool outputs. This is exacerbated by the framework's `streamText` and `generateText` APIs, which prioritize low-latency streaming over built-in sanitization, allowing partial responses to expose data before validation. Agents using tools in `experimental_streamObject` or `streamUI` patterns are particularly vulnerable as unvalidated tool results flow directly into the UI.

howItManifests.scenario: Streaming agent responses with hallucinated secrets
howItManifests.symptom: Partial `streamText` chunks contain API keys or tokens before completion
howItManifests.impact: Real-time UI displays secrets to users during streaming

howItManifests.scenario: Tool call results not sanitized before streaming
howItManifests.symptom: `experimental_streamObject` includes raw database query results with PII
howItManifests.impact: Structured outputs leak customer data in agent responses

howItManifests.scenario: Error handling exposes internal config in agent failures
howItManifests.symptom: Uncaught errors in `generateText` log secrets to client-side console
howItManifests.impact: Browser dev tools capture leaked environment variables

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-provider agent switches leak provider-specific secrets
howItManifests.symptom: Inconsistent `streamText` payloads mix API keys across providers
howItManifests.impact: Cross-provider hallucination exposes multiple credential types

detection.name: Stream Chunk Regex Scanner
detection.description: Intercepts `streamText` chunks and scans for common secret patterns before rendering

detection.name: Tool Output Validator
detection.description: Middleware for `experimental_streamObject` that redacts PII from tool results

detection.name: Error Boundary Logger
detection.description: Captures and sanitizes agent errors that might leak config data

fixes.name: Vex Middleware for All Streams
fixes.description: Wraps `streamText`/`generateText` with automatic PII detection and redaction

fixes.name: Sanitized Tool Schema
fixes.description: Zod schemas that automatically strip sensitive fields from tool outputs

fixes.name: Response Transformer
fixes.description: Post-processes all agent outputs with regex-based redaction

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides production-grade middleware that automatically wraps Vercel AI SDK's `streamText`, `generateText`, and `experimental_streamObject` calls with real-time PII and secret detection. Using pattern-matched detectors trained on millions of agent traces, Vex identifies leaks in streaming chunks, tool outputs, and error messages before they reach users. The SDK integrates in <5 lines of code and provides remediation actions like auto-redaction, stream blocking, and audit logging.

Vex's agent-specific detectors understand Vercel AI SDK patterns including partial hallucinations during streaming, unvalidated tool results, and provider inconsistencies. It automatically classifies leaks by severity (critical: API keys, high: PII, medium: internal config) and enforces your org's data retention policies. Integration happens at the framework level, protecting all agents without code changes.

Remediation is proactive: Vex can block leaky streams, inject redacted fallbacks, or trigger incident workflows. Coverage spans all providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, custom) and deployment targets (Vercel Edge, Next.js). Metrics dashboard shows leakage rates, common patterns, and fix effectiveness across your agent fleet.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add Vex middleware to catch 100% of sensitive data leaks in your Vercel AI SDK agents in under 5 minutes
